channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Red Ants and City Officials Demolish Homes and Small Businesses in Mamelodi
item.description: In late April 2026, the Red Ants, working alongside the South African Police Service and Metro Police, demolished homes and small businesses in the Mamelodi Hostel area. The City of Tshwane claims tha...

item.title: Jerry Hall and major celebrities gather in Scotland as Celebrity Traitors series two begins filming
item.description: Jerry Hall has arrived in Inverness, Scotland, as filming begins for the second series of the BBC’s Celebrity Traitors. The 69-year-old model and actress was seen under tight security shortly after th...

item.title: Saudi Arabia allows children aged 12 and above for Hajj 2026
item.description: KARACHI: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia has reversed its recently announced policy restricting Hajj pilgrims under 15 years of age. Earlier, the Saudi authorities had barred all pilgrims younger than 15 ...

item.title: Moulana Rylands thanks community following Pollsmoor release
item.description: Moulana Mogamat Farouk Rylands, the former imam of the Waterloo Road Mosque in Kenwyn, Cape Town, has expressed his heartfelt gratitude to the local and broader Muslim community following his release ...

item.title: U.S. Alerts Shipping Firms on Risks of Paying Iran for Strait of Hormuz Transit
item.description: The United States has issued a formal warning to international shipping companies regarding payments to Iran for transit through the strategic Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. government emphasized that suc...

item.title: Danny Dyer reflects on his career, personal growth and role in Rivals
item.description: Danny Dyer, the East End actor long known for his tough-guy roles and tabloid notoriety, has reinvented himself as a romantic lead in the TV adaptation of Jilly Cooper’s novel Rivals. At nearly 50, Dy...

item.title: Global Cybercrime Scam Defrauds Victims, Including Canadians, of Over US$200 Million
item.description: A large-scale international scam has defrauded victims worldwide, including Canadians, of nearly US$215 million. Authorities report that the operation involved sophisticated hacking schemes, which dec...

item.title: Nairobi Family Seeks Help After Daughter Goes Missing in Saudi Arabia
item.description: A family from Kibera, Nairobi is deeply worried over their daughter Hellen Ayuma Busiega who has been missing in Saudi Arabia. Ayuma travelled there in August 2022 seeking better job opportunities and...

item.title: Former Manipur Minister AS Arthur Dies at 99 in New Delhi After Prolonged Illness
item.description: Former Manipur minister and ex-MLA AS Arthur passed away early on Saturday morning in New Delhi following a prolonged illness at the age of 99. He is survived by three sons and four daughters. Arthur ...

item.title: Jamie Laing discusses struggles with conceiving first child with Sophie Habboo
item.description: Jamie Laing and Sophie Habboo, stars of Made In Chelsea, recently spoke openly about the challenges they faced when trying to conceive their first child. Appearing on Olivia Attwood's podcast, the cou...

item.title: Court overturns Graham Linehan's conviction over damage to activist's phone
item.description: Graham Linehan, a well-known writer and activist, had his conviction for damaging a transgender activist's mobile phone overturned. The original ruling, which found Linehan guilty, sparked widespread ...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard challenges Chinese vessel near Pag-asa Island during routine mission
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) reported that a Chinese Coast Guard ship allegedly followed a Filipino vessel during a routine mission in the Kalayaan Island Group. PCG Rear Admiral Jay Tarriela conf...

item.title: Prasoon Joshi takes over as Prasar Bharati chairman
item.description: Noted lyricist and writer Prasoon Joshi has been appointed as the new chairman of Prasar Bharati, India’s public service broadcaster, as announced by the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting. Josh...

item.title: Drinking water kiosks set up in Srisailam temple town to ease summer distress
item.description: Authorities in Srisailam have set up drinking water kiosks to help devotees and visitors cope with the summer heat in the temple town. The move is aimed at reducing discomfort for pilgrims who arrive ...

item.title: Tanzania Confirms Tourist Hunting Will Continue with Stronger Regulations
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania government has clarified that there is no plan to ban tourist hunting, instead they are focusing on improving laws, guidelines, and policies to make sure the activity contribu...

item.title: Sanef launches independent inquiry into allegations against Makhudu Sefara
item.description: The South African National Editors’ Forum (Sanef) has initiated an independent legal inquiry following revelations by the Special Investigating Unit (SIU) suggesting that Sunday Times editor and forme...

item.title: Antoinette Lattouf reflects on resilience and advocacy after ABC legal case
item.description: Antoinette Lattouf, a journalist of Lebanese heritage, has spoken openly about the toll of her legal battle with the ABC following her termination after sharing a Human Rights Watch post on Gaza. Whil...

item.title: Gachagua Says Kenyan Workers Struggling with Low Pay and Rising Costs This Labour Day
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has expressed deep concerns about the plight of Kenyan workers in his Labour Day address, arguing that they have little reason to celebrate. He pointed out tha...

item.title: Landstuhl community reacts to US troop reduction plans
item.description: The town of Landstuhl in south-west Germany has a longstanding connection with American soldiers, dating back to the arrival of General George Patton’s Third Army in 1945. This unique US-German identi...

item.title: Trump Faces Growing Challenges as Iran Conflict Drags On
item.description: After more than two months of conflict between the U.S. and Iran, there is no clear resolution in sight, and President Trump faces rising political, economic, and strategic pressures. The war has not ...
